name: Gumiho
description: The Gumiho (구미호), literally "nine-tailed fox," is a creature appearing in Korean folktales and legends. It is typically portrayed as a fox that has lived for a thousand years and possesses the ability to shapeshift, often transforming into a beautiful woman to seduce and deceive men. While some Gumihos are depicted as purely malevolent beings who crave human livers or hearts for sustenance and to maintain their transformation, others are portrayed as having more complex motivations, sometimes even desiring to become fully human themselves. The process of becoming human is often described as a difficult and arduous task, requiring the Gumiho to abstain from eating human flesh for a thousand days or to perform good deeds. The Gumiho's power is tied to its nine tails, which represent its age and accumulated magic. The more tails a fox has, the more powerful it is. Some tales depict the Gumiho as possessing supernatural abilities such as illusion, mind control, and enhanced senses. The Gumiho remains a popular figure in modern Korean culture, appearing in numerous films, television dramas, and video games, often with varying interpretations of its nature and motivations.
subtitle: The alluring nine-tailed fox spirit
appearance: The Gumiho typically appears as a beautiful woman, often possessing captivating eyes and an alluring aura. In its true form, it is a fox with nine tails, each representing centuries of life and accumulated power. Its fur can range in color from white to silver to golden, and its eyes often glow with an eerie light. Some depictions show the Gumiho with sharp claws and teeth, hinting at its predatory nature.
habitat: Gumihos are often associated with remote mountains, forests, and ancient ruins, where they can live undisturbed and practice their magic. They may also infiltrate human society, living in disguise among unsuspecting people.
behavior: The Gumiho's behavior varies depending on the specific tale. Some are depicted as purely evil creatures, driven by a thirst for human flesh and a desire to cause chaos. Others are portrayed as more complex beings, with a longing for humanity and a desire to escape their cursed existence. They may use their beauty and charm to seduce and manipulate humans, but their ultimate goals often remain shrouded in mystery.

keyMyths.title: The Gumiho's Hunger
keyMyths.description: Many tales depict the Gumiho as needing to consume human livers or hearts to maintain its human form and power. This hunger drives their interactions with humans and often leads to tragedy.

keyMyths.title: The Path to Humanity
keyMyths.description: Some stories offer the Gumiho a path to becoming human, often requiring them to abstain from eating human flesh for a thousand days or to perform acts of kindness and compassion.
